text.title: structural-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for structural-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Facade that grows into a God Object (50+ methods, 1000+ lines)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A large Facade is just a different form of coupling — all clients depend on one giant class. Split into multiple focused facades or redesign the subsystem.

failure-mode.description: Applying Adapter when Facade is needed (or vice versa)
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Adapter converts a single incompatible interface; Facade simplifies a multi-class subsystem. Using Adapter for a complex subsystem creates a thin wrapper that still exposes subsystem complexity. Using Facade for an interface mismatch obscures the actual conversion logic.
